zh.lifeSummary: 當William Laith de Burgh於1260年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威時，他的父親Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight30歲，母親Agnes Ô’Brien26歲。他於1290年與Finnoula O'Brien在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1294年登記服兵役。他於1308年被列為custos or warden of ireland。他去世於1324年2月13日在愛爾蘭享年64歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威阿騰利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Laith de Burgh于1260年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 高威时，他的父亲Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight30岁，母亲Agnes Ô’Brien26岁。他于1290年与Finnoula O'Brien在爱尔兰庫迪 高威结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1294年登记服兵役。他于1308年被列为custos or warden of ireland。他去世于1324年2月13日在爱尔兰享年64岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 高威阿騰利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Laith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun denoting the quality of believing and trusting in God. The name began to be used in the 16th century, was very popular among the Puritans of the 17th, and is presently enjoying a modest comeback.

en.lifeSummary: When William Laith de Burgh was born in 1260, in County Galway, Ireland, his father, Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight, was 30 and his mother, Agnes Ô’Brien, was 26. He married Finnoula O'Brien in 1290, in County Galway, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1294. In 1308, at the age of 48, his occupation is listed as custos or warden of ireland. He died on 13 February 1324, in Ireland, at the age of 64, and was buried in Athenry, County Galway, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Laith de Burgh est né en 1260, à County Galway, Irlande, son père, Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agnes Ô’Brien, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Finnoula O'Brien en 1290, à County Galway, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1294. En 1308, à l’âge de 48 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est custos or warden of ireland. Il est décédé le 13 février 1324, à Irlande, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Athenry, County Galway, Irlande.

de.Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irel: Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irland
de.lifeSummary: Als William Laith de Burgh 1260 in County Galway, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight 30 und seine Mutter Agnes Ô’Brien 26. Er hat 1290 in County Galway, Irland Finnoula O'Brien geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 11 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1294 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1308, als er 48 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als custos or warden of ireland aufgeführt. Er starb am 13. Februar 1324 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Irland und wurde in Athenry, County Galway, Irland begraben.

Lui sposato Finnoula O'Brien nel 1290, nel County Galway, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 11 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1294. Nel 1308, all'età di 48 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come custos or warden of ireland. Morì il 13 febbraio 1324, nel Irlanda, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: William Laith de Burghが1260年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイで生まれた時，父Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knightは30歳，母Agnes Ô’Brienは26歳であった。1290年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイでFinnoula O'Brienと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1294年に兵役登録をした。1308年，48歳の時，職業はcustos or warden of irelandと記録されている。彼は1324年2月13日にアイルランドで64歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ガルワイアスエンリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Laith de Burgh(이)가 1260년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Ô’Brien(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1290년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이에서 Finnoula O'Brien(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1294년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1308년, 48세의 나이로 그의 직업은 custos or warden of ireland이었다. 그는 1324년 2월 13일 아일랜드에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 앳헨리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Laith de Burgh nasceu em 1260, em County Galway, Irlanda, seu pai, Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Ô’Brien, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Finnoula O'Brien em 1290, em County Galway, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 11 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1294. Em 1308, com 48 anos, sua profissão aparece como custos or warden of ireland. Ele faleceu em 13 de fevereiro de 1324, em Irlanda, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ru.Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irel: каррикк он шеннон, коунти литрим, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Laith de Burgh родился в 1260 г. в местности коунти галвеи, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Ô’Brien, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Finnoula O'Brien в 1290 г. в местности коунти галвеи, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 1 дочь. В 1294 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1308 году, в возрасте 48 лет, работал по профессии custos or warden of ireland. Он умер 13 февраля 1324 г. в местности Ирландия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности афенри, коунти галвеи, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Laith de Burgh nació en 1260, en County Galway, Irlanda, su papá, Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Agnes Ô’Brien, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Finnoula O'Brien en 1290, en County Galway, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1294. En 1308, a la edad de 48 años, su ocupación registrada era custos or warden of ireland. Murió el 13 de febrero de 1324, en Irlanda, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.
